## Structure

UDP Cв†’S 0x03/0x31 вЂ” `RequestShortPlayerInfo`. Client's
follow-up probe asking the server to (re-)send `ShortPlayerInfo`
(0x03/0x30) for a specific in-zone player. Fixed 6-byte body.
Verified 2026-05-10 against `RequestShortPlayerInfo.java`
matching all 18 retail samples.

```
[0..3]    skip 4 bytes              parser skips first 4 bytes
                                    (likely a sub-header / flags
                                    field вЂ” content varies)
[4..5]    map_id LE16               in-zone player index/slot
[??]      remainder                 trailing zeros to total 6B
```

Sample retail bytes:
```
02 00 02 00 00 00     map_id = 0x0002
03 00 03 00 00 00     map_id = 0x0003
01 00 01 00 00 00     map_id = 0x0001
```

The first 2 bytes appear to mirror the map_id (LE16) too вЂ”
so retail payload is `[map_id LE16][map_id LE16][00 00]` with
the parser ignoring the duplicate via `skip(4)` before reading
the second `mapId`.

## Variants

Single 6-byte form. The duplicated map_id pattern is byte-stable
across all 18 retail samples; the trailing 2 zero bytes are
constant.

## Observed contexts

Emitted when the client wants info about a specific in-zone
player (by their map_id slot). Concentrated in PvP-heavy
captures (`VEHICLE_DRONE` Г— 6, LONG_PARTY Г— 3 each) вЂ” clients
proactively probe nearby players.

The `RequestShortPlayerInfo`/`ShortPlayerInfo` round-trip is
the lighter-weight equivalent of the `LongPlayerInfo` flow:
client asks for a specific player by slot, server returns
just name + ID.

## Open questions

- Why duplicate the map_id LE16 in the first 4 bytes that
  `skip(4)` discards? Possibly the client's parser writes
  `[u32 padding=mapId LE32][LE16 mapId]` вЂ” i.e. the same
  value redundantly stored as both LE32 and LE16.
- The 2 trailing zero bytes after map_id: reserved for an
  additional flag (e.g. include-equipment-info)?

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.client_udp.RequestShortPlayerInfo`
вЂ” parses the 6-byte body, looks up the target player by
`mapId` slot, and emits `ShortPlayerInfo` (0x03/0x30) back
with the queried player's data.

Implementation:
```java
skip(4); // unknown / duplicate
int mapId = readShort();
Player pl2 = pl.getZone().getPlayer(mapId);
if (pl2 != null)
    pl.send(new ShortPlayerInfo(pl, pl2.getCharacter(), mapId));
```

Edge case: if `mapId` doesn't resolve to a player in the zone,
the server silently drops the request (no error response).
